assertions.scriptName: EXCLUDED.col copies the new value into the existing row
assertions.query: SELECT id, name, age FROM users WHERE id = 1;

assertions.scriptName: EXCLUDED in expressions and mixed with existing column refs
assertions.query: SELECT id, hits, label FROM counters WHERE id = 1;

assertions.scriptName: EXCLUDED case-insensitive (lowercase, uppercase, mixed)
assertions.query: SELECT v FROM c_t WHERE id = 1;

assertions.scriptName: EXCLUDED case-insensitive (lowercase, uppercase, mixed)
assertions.query: SELECT v FROM c_t WHERE id = 1;

assertions.scriptName: EXCLUDED with multi-row VALUES applies the matched row
assertions.query: SELECT id, v FROM m ORDER BY id;

assertions.scriptName: Zero permissions singleton initializer
assertions.query: SELECT lock::text, (permissions IS NULL)::text FROM zero_permissions_default_probe;

assertions.scriptName: Zero permissions singleton initializer
assertions.query: SELECT count(*)::text, bool_and(lock)::text FROM zero_permissions_default_probe;

assertions.scriptName: Zero permissions singleton initializer with hash trigger
assertions.query: SELECT count(*)::text, bool_and("lock")::text, bool_and("hash" IS NULL)::text FROM zero_permissions_trigger_probe;

assertions.scriptName: BEFORE INSERT triggers see omitted defaults with explicit column mapping
assertions.query:
SELECT id, marker, supplied, nullable_default, noted
						FROM trigger_default_order_probe;

assertions.scriptName: WHERE true applies the update
assertions.query: SELECT v FROM w WHERE id = 1;

assertions.scriptName: WHERE false leaves the existing row unchanged, no error
assertions.query: SELECT v FROM w2 WHERE id = 1;

assertions.scriptName: WHERE referencing EXCLUDED
assertions.query: SELECT v FROM w3 WHERE id = 1;

assertions.scriptName: WHERE referencing EXCLUDED
assertions.query: SELECT v FROM w3 WHERE id = 1;

assertions.scriptName: WHERE in mixed multi-row insert: each row checked independently
assertions.query: SELECT id, v FROM w4 ORDER BY id;

assertions.scriptName: ON CONFLICT (pk) on table with email UNIQUE: target conflict updates
assertions.query: SELECT id, email, name FROM u WHERE id = 1;

assertions.scriptName: ON CONFLICT (pk): non-target unique conflict raises
assertions.query:
INSERT INTO u2 (id, email, name) VALUES (3, 'a@x.com', 'wrong')
ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE SET name = EXCLUDED.name;

assertions.scriptName: ON CONFLICT (pk): non-target unique conflict raises
assertions.query: SELECT id, email, name FROM u2 ORDER BY id;

assertions.scriptName: ON CONFLICT (email): same coverage from the other unique direction
assertions.query: SELECT id, email, name FROM u3;

assertions.scriptName: ON CONFLICT (email): same coverage from the other unique direction
assertions.query:
INSERT INTO u3 (id, email, name) VALUES (1, 'fresh@x.com', 'wrong')
ON CONFLICT (email) DO UPDATE SET name = EXCLUDED.name;

assertions.scriptName: ON CONFLICT (pk) DO NOTHING with multi-unique: target conflict ignored
assertions.query: SELECT id, email, name FROM u4;

assertions.scriptName: ON CONFLICT (pk) DO NOTHING raises on non-target unique conflict
assertions.query: INSERT INTO u4b (id, email, name) VALUES (2, 'a@x.com', 'wrong') ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;

assertions.scriptName: ON CONFLICT (pk) DO NOTHING raises on non-target unique conflict
assertions.query: SELECT id, email, name FROM u4b ORDER BY id;

assertions.scriptName: ON CONFLICT (email) on table with id PK + email UNIQUE (2 seed rows)
assertions.query: SELECT id, email, name FROM u_two ORDER BY id;

assertions.scriptName: ON CONFLICT (col) WHERE pred parses and routes through target
assertions.query: SELECT v FROM arb_t WHERE id = 1;

assertions.scriptName: ON CONFLICT (col) WHERE pred parses and routes through target
assertions.query: SELECT v FROM arb_t WHERE id = 1;

ON CONFLICT (user_id) WHERE status = 'inactive' DO NOTHING;

assertions.query:

ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;
